title: Magical Nutty Ethiopian Almond Tagine
description: A kid-friendly Ethiopian dish featuring ground almonds and lardons-render, this recipe is both nutty and gooey, with a low-calorie twist.
instructions: Heat a large Dutch oven over medium heat and add the diced lardons to render the fat.
Remove the lardons from the pot and set aside, leaving the rendered fat behind.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the pot and cook for an additional minute.
Stir in the berbere spice and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the ground almonds to the pot and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly, until lightly toasted.
Add the chicken broth, honey, salt, and black pepper to the pot and stir to combine.
Return the lardons to the pot and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 20 minutes, until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
